prompt:
You're keeping a small log of incoming invoices in a spreadsheet.

Open the email app at http://cua_email.web and sign in as user_0001 (enter the user id and create the session). Find the invoice from Vendor Billing with the subject "Invoice #2024-001" — searching the inbox for the sender is the quickest way to find it. You can read everything you need straight from the inbox row, so there's no need to open the message, and please leave the emails untouched.

Then switch to the spreadsheet at http://cua_spreadsheet.web (the "Book 1" workbook is already open) and record the invoice as a two-column table. Down column A put the labels Sender, Subject, Folder, Year and Invoice no; in column B put the matching values — Vendor Billing, the subject "Invoice #2024-001", the folder Inbox, the year taken from the invoice number (2024), and the part after the # (2024-001). Match the text exactly.

The quickest way to fill it in is to click A1 and type the whole table in one go, tabbing between the two columns and pressing Enter at the end of each row, rather than editing the cells one by one.

source.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM cells WHERE row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender'
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM cells WHERE row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Vendor Billing'
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM cells WHERE row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject'
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM cells WHERE row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Invoice #2024-001'

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Year' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND (raw_value='2024' OR computed_value='2024') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Invoice no' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a5,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='2024-001' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b5
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='Vendor Billing' AND subject='Invoice #2024-001'

prompt:
You're jotting down a few details about a meeting invite in a spreadsheet.

Open the email app at http://cua_email.web and sign in as user_0001. Find the message from David Kim about the team lunch this Friday (subject "Team Lunch - This Friday") — searching the inbox for the sender is the fastest way to find it. The sender and subject are right there in the inbox row, so you don't need to open it, and leave the emails as they are.

Then switch to the spreadsheet at http://cua_spreadsheet.web (the "Book 1" workbook is already open) and record it as a two-column table. Down column A put the labels Sender, Subject, Folder, Day and Event; in column B the matching values — David Kim, the subject of that email, the folder Inbox, the weekday mentioned in the subject (Friday), and the kind of event in a single word (Lunch).

The quickest way to fill it in is to click A1 and type the whole table in one go, tabbing between the columns and pressing Enter at the end of each row, rather than filling the cells one at a time.

source.query:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a1,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='David Kim' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b1
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a2,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value LIKE '%Lunch%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b2
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Day' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='friday' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Event' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a5,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='lunch' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b5
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='David Kim' AND subject='Team Lunch - This Friday'

prompt:
You're noting down a meeting from your inbox in a spreadsheet.

Open the email app at http://cua_email.web and sign in as user_0001. Find the message from Michael Chen about the design review meeting (subject "Design Review Meeting - February") — searching the inbox for the sender is the fastest way to find it. You can read the sender and subject from the inbox row without opening it, and please don't change any emails.

Then switch to the spreadsheet at http://cua_spreadsheet.web (the "Book 1" workbook is already open) and record it as a two-column table. Down column A put the labels Sender, Subject, Folder, Month and Topic; in column B the matching values — Michael Chen, the full subject exactly ("Design Review Meeting - February"), the folder Inbox, the month named in the subject (February), and the first word of the subject (Design).

The quickest way to fill it in is to click A1 and type the whole table in one go, tabbing between the columns and pressing Enter at the end of each row, rather than editing the cells individually.

source.query:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender'       THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a1,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Michael Chen' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b1
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a2,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Design Review Meeting - February' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b2
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Month'    THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='February' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Topic' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a5,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='design' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b5
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='Michael Chen' AND subject='Design Review Meeting - February'

prompt:
You're keeping a small log of documents people have sent you.

Open the email app at http://cua_email.web and sign in as user_0001. Find the message from Jessica Martinez with the subject "Marketing Campaign Proposal" — searching the inbox for the sender is the quickest way to find it. The sender and subject show in the inbox row, so there's no need to open it, and leave the emails untouched.

Then switch to the spreadsheet at http://cua_spreadsheet.web (the "Book 1" workbook is already open) and record it as a two-column table. Down column A put the labels Sender, Subject, Folder, Topic and DocType; in column B the matching values — Jessica Martinez, the subject of that email, the folder Inbox, the first word of the subject (Marketing), and the last word of the subject, which is the document type (Proposal).

Fill it in one go: click A1 and type all the rows as a single block, with a Tab between the two columns and Enter after each row. Then check the result — if any cell came out empty, click that exact cell and type only its value. Don't use Enter to move from one cell to the next when fixing things, because that shifts the rows down and mislays the last value.

source.query:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a1,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Jessica Martinez' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b1
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a2,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value LIKE '%Marketing%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b2
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Topic' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='marketing' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='DocType' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a5,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='proposal' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b5
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='Jessica Martinez' AND subject='Marketing Campaign Proposal'

prompt:
You're keeping a small log of follow-up notes from your inbox.

Open the email app at http://cua_email.web and sign in as user_0001. Find the message from Carlos Mendez about the incident retrospective notes (subject "Re: Incident retrospective notes") — searching the inbox for the sender is the fastest way to find it. The sender and subject are visible in the inbox row, so there's no need to open it, and please leave the emails untouched.

Then switch to the spreadsheet at http://cua_spreadsheet.web (the "Book 1" workbook is already open) and record it as a two-column table. Down column A put the labels Sender, Subject, Folder, Topic and DocType; in column B the matching values — Carlos Mendez, the exact subject including the "Re:" prefix, the folder Inbox, the first word after "Re:" (Incident), and the last word of the subject (notes). Match the subject text exactly.

Fill it in one go: click A1 and type all the rows as a single block, with a Tab between the two columns and Enter after each row. Then check the result — if any cell came out empty, click that exact cell and type only its value. Don't use Enter to move from one cell to the next when fixing things, because that shifts the rows down and mislays the last value.

source.query:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a1,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Carlos Mendez' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b1
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a2,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Re: Incident retrospective notes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b2
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Topic' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='incident' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='DocType' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a5,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='notes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b5
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='Carlos Mendez' AND subject='Re: Incident retrospective notes'
